_comment: Sanitized example matching the real Horizon config shape used by this fork's CI (see data/config.github.json in the private repo, copied to the runtime config path on each run). No secrets here: the AI provider key and Feishu credentials are GitHub Actions repository secrets, read at runtime via the *_env names below — never committed as literal values. Categorization (which section a rendered item lands in) is NOT configured here; it's decided in code by scripts/render_html.py (SOURCE_CAT / CATEGORIES / CAT_ORDER). Tune sources/thresholds to taste.

sources._github_note: 10 repo `releases` watchers + ~9 topic `search` queries in the real config; releases are disabled by default (repo_releases can be noisy), search entries carry per-topic min_stars/created_within_days floors. Shown here: 2 of each as a representative sample.

ossinsight._comment: keywords is a substring filter over GitHub's trending feed; real config carries ~23 keywords, shortened here.

sources._rss_note: 6 representative feeds shown below (one per category); the real config carries ~53 curated feeds total, see data/config.github.json — spanning vendor / industry+digest / hardware / supply-chain / independent-blog categories.

filtering._comment: ai_score_threshold: 0-10, higher = fewer/higher-signal items (~6 = a readable daily digest, drop to ~4 on slow days). time_window_hours: lookback window for time-bounded sources. github_min_items: if at-threshold GitHub items are fewer than this, top up with below-threshold ones down to github_floor_min_score (the 'quality floor'). seen_suppress_days: cross-day dedup window in days, per source, checked against the committed seen-store.

webhook._comment: Inherited from upstream Horizon's generic card-webhook delivery path — this fork does NOT use it. Actual delivery is a CI step running scripts/push_file_feishu.py, which uploads the rendered HTML report as a file plus a short TOP5 text intro. Leave this block disabled unless you want the upstream webhook-card path instead.
